paper_title: Effect of the surface mounting technology assembly based on lean production: A case study

entities.description: Author from the Department of Industrial Management, Can Tho University.
Author from the Department of Electrical Engineering, Can Tho University.
Educational institution in Vietnam where the research was conducted.
The primary field of research concerning production systems and technology.
A methodology focused on eliminating waste and enhancing effectiveness.
A method for producing electronic circuits in which the components are mounted directly onto the surface of printed circuit boards.
The process of eliminating non-value-added activities in production.
A lean-management method for analyzing the current state and designing a future state for the series of events that take a product or service from its beginning through to the customer.
A workplace organization method that uses a list of five Japanese words: seiri, seiton, seiso, seiketsu, and shitsuke.
A layout where machines are grouped into cells to maximize independence and flow.
A performance rating system used for setting standard times.
Equipment used to apply solder paste to pads on a PCB.
High-speed machines used for placing components onto PCBs.
A machine used for forming electrical solder connections between components and PCB.
The recorded improvement in production output per shift.

entities.id: Lead time reduction (6 days to 192.76 seconds)
entities.description: The decrease in the time taken from the start to the end of the production process.
The improvement in the ratio of completed products on the line.
The rate at which a finished product needs to be completed in order to meet customer demand.
The numerical data collected from the company's production line and customer demands.

relations.source: Effect of the surface mounting technology assembly based on lean production: A case study

relations.target: Lead time reduction (6 days to 192.76 seconds)
